items.text:
Notice: Bicycle Registration Program

The Office of Campus Safety will open bicycle registration for the fall semester from September 8 through September 26. All students who keep a bicycle on campus must register it at the Transportation Center in Wheeler Hall between 9:00 AM and 4:00 PM on weekdays.

Registration is free for the first bicycle. A $5 fee applies to each additional bicycle registered under the same student account. To complete the process, please bring the following items:

- A valid student ID card
- The bicycle's serial number
- A completed registration form, available online through the campus portal

Once registered, each bicycle will receive a numbered decal that must be attached to the frame. Unregistered bicycles found locked to campus racks after October 1 may be removed and held in storage. A $10 release fee will be charged to recover any impounded bicycle.

Questions may be directed to the Transportation Center at extension 3120 or safety@campus.edu.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what must a student bring to register a bicycle?
questions.explanation: The notice lists a valid student ID card among the items required to complete registration; the other options are plausible campus documents that the notice never mentions.

questions.stem: How much is charged to recover an impounded bicycle?
questions.explanation: The notice states that a $10 release fee will be charged to recover any impounded bicycle. The $5 figure is the fee for an additional bicycle, used here as a distractor.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about bicycles left unregistered after October 1?

options.A: They will receive a numbered decal
options.B: They are eligible for a partial refund
options.C: They may be removed from campus racks
options.D: They must be insured before storage

questions.explanation: The notice says unregistered bicycles found locked to racks after October 1 may be removed and held in storage, so an unregistered bicycle risks removal. A numbered decal goes only to registered bicycles.
